title: Budget Estimate Request — Wenger Not Listed
openingLine: Hey, I sent you an email with a link to the bid docs for the Westridge new school project. I need a budget estimate. Wenger isn't named in the docs but I think there's some music casework in there — figured I'd check.
customerContext: You're handling preconstruction for a new K-8 school. The architect didn't spec Wenger — they used generic 'music casework' language. You don't really know the music product space. You're treating Wenger as one of several possible suppliers and you're trying to get budget numbers for an early estimate.

hiddenInfo.reveal: $45M new K-8 school. Music spaces include band, choir, and a small practice room.
Drawings show a music storage room — about 250 SF — but no detailed casework spec.
Not listed by name. Spec says 'music storage casework — by general casework supplier.'
I don't know if the district has bought Wenger before. I'm new to this district.
Owner has flagged that they want VE alternates. Music storage might be a candidate to upgrade.
Architect is Hodges + Shea. They've been responsive to substitution requests on past projects.
Budget estimate due to me in 10 days. Bid is in 6 weeks.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Called the CM directly to confirm scope
Checked Salesforce for prior contacts or conversations with district owners
Asked whether the district has previously purchased Wenger cabinets
Assessed whether this could be a VE product line

Solution Framing.behavior: Explained Wenger's solutions and value vs. generic casework
Requested Wenger product be included in the bid
Connected with the architect to lock in spec language

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Followed up with the CM for a connection if no initial response

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Aligned response to the 10-day budget estimate window

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Established next step with the CM and architect
